summary: This page explains how to combine two sinusoids of the same frequency, of the form a cos(ω0 t) + b sin(ω0 t), into a single cosine C cos(ω0 t + θ). It gives formulas for the amplitude and phase using the magnitude and angle of the complex number a - jb, illustrates the result with phasor addition, and warns about correctly determining the quadrant of θ. The page also begins Example B.6, applying the method to express specific sinusoidal combinations as a single sinusoid.

figures.caption: Fig. B.7 Phasor addition of sinusoids.
